name: Pawn Max II
description_short: Pawn Max II is a pawn shop in Pinellas Park, FL offering collateral-based loans and buy/sell of used goods, with extended hours six days a week.
description_long:
Pawn Max II operates at 7561 49th St N in Pinellas Park, Florida, serving the greater St. Petersburg and Clearwater area of Pinellas County. The shop's URL structure (pm4.866pawn.com) indicates it is the fourth location in the 866 Pawn chain, suggesting a multi-location regional operation rather than a standalone independent shop. The Pinellas Park location sits in a commercially dense corridor near major retailers including Walmart, Target, and The Home Depot, making it accessible for residents across the county.

As a pawn shop, Pawn Max II's core offering is collateral-based lending: customers bring in items of value — electronics, jewelry, tools, musical instruments, firearms (where permitted) — and receive a short-term loan secured by that item. If the loan plus fees is repaid within the redemption period, the customer reclaims their property. If not, the shop takes ownership and resells the item. Beyond loans, pawn shops in this chain typically also buy outright and resell used merchandise across multiple categories.

What distinguishes Pawn Max II operationally is its extended business hours. The shop is open Monday through Saturday from 9:00 AM to 8:00 PM and Sunday from 10:00 AM to 6:00 PM — seven days a week with late weeknight hours. This is notably more accessible than many competitors that close by 6:00 PM or are closed Sundays. Being part of the 866 Pawn network also likely means standardized procedures and potentially consistent loan terms across locations.

Pawn shops serve a genuine need for consumers who need fast access to cash without a credit check and do not qualify for or want traditional loan products. However, pawn loan interest rates and fees in Florida, while state-regulated, are typically far higher than conventional consumer credit on an APR basis. Borrowers should treat a pawn loan as a short-term emergency measure and have a clear plan to reclaim their item within the redemption window.
pros: Extended weekday hours (9:00 AM – 8:00 PM Monday through Friday)
Open seven days a week including Sundays (10:00 AM – 6:00 PM)
Saturday hours until 8:00 PM for after-work accessibility
Part of the multi-location 866 Pawn chain, suggesting standardized processes
Physical storefront with a dedicated local phone number: (727) 547-1800
Located in a high-traffic commercial area with easy access near Walmart and Target
cons: Pawn loan rates in Florida can be high — state law caps fees but effective APR is often triple digits
Limited publicly available information about specific loan terms, rates, or maximum loan amounts
Items not redeemed within the loan period are forfeited and resold — no partial redemption
Single location; no online or remote service options apparent from available data
Customer reviews and complaint history are not surfaced from the available source
best_for: Consumers who need fast cash without a credit check and own items of resale value
Pinellas County residents seeking a pawn shop with evening and Sunday availability
Buyers looking for discounted used electronics, tools, or jewelry at a resale shop
People who need a short-term bridge loan and are confident they can repay within the redemption period
diagnosis: Pawn Max II is best for Pinellas County residents who need same-day cash without a credit check and have an asset they can temporarily part with — the extended seven-day hours make it more accessible than most competitors. The main caveat is that pawn loan fees are expensive on an APR basis, and failing to redeem the item means permanent loss of the collateral, so this should be a last resort rather than a routine borrowing tool.
services: Collateral-based pawn loans (items held as security)
Item buyouts (outright purchase of customer property)
In-store cash disbursement (same-day, no credit check)
